channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Time to evacuate is running out as Hurricane Milton closes in on Florida
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton churned Wednesday toward a potentially catastrophic collision along the west coast of Florida, where some residents insisted they would stay after millions were ordered to evacuate and officials warned that stragglers would face grim odds of surviving.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court takes up death row case with a rare alliance. Oklahoma inmate has state's support
item.description: The Supreme Court is returning to the case of Richard Glossip, who has spent most of the past quarter century on Oklahoma’s death row for a murder he says he did not commit.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zillow feature: Climate risk data added to home listings
item.description: A recent survey reported that more than four out of five prospective homebuyers consider climate risks when pondering where to live.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton takes aim near Tampa Bay, Florida, once again a Category 5 storm
item.description: Hurricane Milton remains a ferocious storm that could land a once-in-a-century direct hit on Tampa and St. Petersburg, engulfing the populous region with towering storm surges and turning debris from Helene’s devastation 12 days ago into projectiles. While the storm had previously weakened, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NC National Guard Black Hawk crew grounded in 'rotor wash' incident over Helene aid
item.description: Guard officials said the aircraft was a North Carolina National Guard UH-60 Black Hawk helicopter making a delivery of generators -- as video indicated it was in Burnsville.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Riley Keough felt a duty to finish Lisa Marie Presley’s book on Elvis, grief, addiction and love
item.description: Riley Keough was quick to agree to help complete her mother’s memoir. She thought they’d write it together, reflecting on her extraordinary upbringing and life, but it became a much greater responsibility after Lisa Marie Presley’ssudden death in 2023.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rough ride: NOAA hurricane hunters fly into eye of Milton in heart-racing flight caught on camera
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration sent aircraft missions on into the eye of Hurricane Milton to collect data on hurricanes as the cyclone barrels towards Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: All four of the passengers on board, including a dog, were rescued by the police department in a small boat.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Milton
item.description: Researchers on a hurricane hunter aircraft had a bumpy ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Afghan man in Oklahoma plotted Election Day terror attack in US on behalf of ISIS, Justice Department says
item.description: An Afghan man has been charged with plotting a terror attack on Election Day on behalf of the Islamic State terror group, the FBI said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How do officials plan to manage CRB infestations on Oahu?
item.description: “It’s another sad day for us, we don’t like removing trees, especially trees like the coconut," said Roxanne Adams, DPR Urban Forestry administrator.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cervantes, NewsNation

item.title: CENTCOM to continue supporting Middle East operations as Hurricane Milton barrels toward Florida headquarters
item.description: CENTCOM personnel are relocating to back up sites to be able to continue supporting 24/7 operations in the Middle East and elsewhere as Hurricane Milton barrels toward its headquarters in Tampa, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden sets 10-year deadline for US cities to replace lead pipes nationwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A decade after the Flint, Michigan, water crisis raised alarms about the continuing dangers of lead in tap water, President Joe Biden on Tuesday set a 10-year deadline for cities across the nation to replace their lead pipes, finalizing an aggressive approach aimed at ensuring that
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: West Virginia woman met her husband after getting donor lungs from his late sister
item.description: A woman from West Virginia got a new chance at life and found the love of her life thanks to organ donation.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, Fox 5

item.title: Israel moves into southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel is expanding its efforts by invading southern Lebanon and says it killed a senior Hezbollah commander in a strike on Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, KTLA 5

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: Severe weather can target any part of the globe, but each year the Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic devastation to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court to decide whether Oklahoma must execute death row inmate
item.description: Richard Glossip, an Oklahoma death row inmate, has been scheduled for execution nine times — and each time, he has avoided that fate following legal twists and turns. Convicted for the 1997 killing of his former boss, Glossip has maintained his innocence for nearly three decades.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canyoneer dies after accidental 150-foot fall at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2025 predicted to be lower than 2024
item.description: The Social Security Administration will announce a new cost-of-living adjustment on Oct. 10, 2024, with the increase expected to be smaller than last year.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Al Pacino claims he almost died of COVID: 'I was gone'
item.description: During a podcast appearance on The New York Times’ “The Interview," Al Pacino claims he once nearly died of COVID.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Another top aide to New York City mayor resigns amid federal probe
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams said Monday that he’s accepted the resignation of his deputy mayor for public safety, Philip Banks, the latest senior official to leave as the mayor fends off an indictment and calls to step down.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KAREN MATTHEWS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states. Helene came ashore Sept.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Milton forms in the Gulf, NHC says
item.description: Tropical Depression Fourteen has strengthened into Tropical Storm Milton, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, WGN 9

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
